input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-28T00:59:08.482Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

arguments.description: Run test executable directly to confirm exit code 8

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run test executable directly to confirm exit code 8

data.reasoningText:
I can see the issue - with Microsoft.Testing.Platform, when there are zero tests, it reports "Failed!" even though no tests actually failed. The exit code from `dotnet test` wrapper might be 0, but if they run the test executable directly, they'd get exit code 8.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>false</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>` or more precisely, the right property to handle zero-test scenarios.

Actually, the correct solution for Microsoft.Testing.Platform is to use the `--minimum-expected-tests` option or the corresponding MSBuild property. By default, Microsoft.Testing.Platform considers 0 tests as a failure (exit code 8). 


I can pass `--minimum-expected-tests 0` either through the command line with `-- --minimum-expected-tests 0` or by setting it as an MSBuild property in the csproj file, which should tell the test runner to accept zero tests as a valid outcome rather than treating it as a failure condition.

data.content:
Exit code 8 confirmed — Microsoft.Testing.Platform treats "zero tests discovered" as a failure, unlike VSTest.

The fix is the `--minimum-expected-tests 0` option. You can set it in the `.csproj` so it applies everywhere:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow test assemblies to pass even when conditional compilation produces zero tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow test assemblies to pass even when conditional compilation produces zero tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-a33833cc3e9c43f18a8a80caef59c4b4/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow test assemblies to pass even when conditional compilation produces zero tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow test assemblies to pass even when conditional compilation produces zero tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText: It seems the `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` MSBuild property doesn't propagate when running the executable directly. That makes sense - it's only picked up by `dotnet test`. Let me try passing it directly on the command line.

data.content: The `--minimum-expected-tests` doesn't accept 0. But the help shows `--ignore-exit-code 8` is the right approach. Let me update:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow test assemblies to pass even when conditional compilation produces zero tests -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = zero tests discovered; ignore it for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
`dotnet test` now returns exit code **0** with the zero-tests scenario. Notice it still says "Failed!" in the summary line but immediately follows with "Tests succeeded" — the `--ignore-exit-code 8` property is working.

Let me also confirm that actual test failures still fail properly:

     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
     <!-- Exit code 8 = zero tests discovered; ignore it for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content:
**The fix:** Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` via the `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>` MSBuild property.

Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). The property tells `dotnet test` to pass `--ignore-exit-code 8` to the test runner, converting that specific exit code to 0.

- ✅ Zero tests → exit code 0 (no CI failure)  
- ✅ Real test pass → exit code 0  
- ✅ Real test failures still produce a non-zero exit code  

If you have many test projects, you can put this property in a `Directory.Build.props` instead.
